subject: Social Science
overall_learning_objectives: Know about historical struggles against discrimination
Learn about contributions of social reformers
Relate past struggles to fairness in daily life
Develop empathy, observation, and discussion skills

lesson_plan.title: What is Equality?
student_activities: Share a time they felt treated unfairly
Give examples of fairness in school or home

lesson_plan.title: Struggles Against Discrimination
teaching_strategy: Use pictures or short stories of reformers
student_activities: Identify examples of discrimination from the stories

lesson_plan.title: Social Reformers and Activists
topics_covered: Introduction to reformers like Jyotirao Phule, B.R. Ambedkar, and others
teaching_strategy: Show images and tell short stories
student_activities: Name one reformer and their work
Draw a small portrait or symbol representing them

lesson_plan.title: Modern Examples of Equality
topics_covered: How schools and laws promote fairness today
teaching_strategy: Ask students questions about rules in school or home
student_activities: List examples of equality in school
Discuss why rules are needed for fairness
assessment: Short written list or oral response

lesson_plan.title: Activities and Stories
topics_covered: Simple stories of people fighting for equality
teaching_strategy: Read short stories or narrate incidents
student_activities: Share which story inspired them most
Draw a scene showing fairness or justice

lesson_plan.title: Revision and Fun Quiz
topics_covered: Relate fairness to students’ own life
teaching_strategy: Fun quiz with pictures and multiple choice
student_activities: Draw or share one lesson learned

teacher_tip: Keep the lesson visual and interactive. Use simple stories, pictures, and discussions. Encourage students to talk about fairness in their own life and express opinions.
